data.title: Crypto Safe Harbor Framework Advances to White House Review
data.description: A proposed crypto safe harbor framework has progressed to the White House's Office of Information and Regulatory Affairs (OIRA) for review, according to SEC Chair Paul Atkins. The framework is expected to be published soon. According to NS3.AI, the proposal includes a startup exemption allowing crypto stakeholders to raise capital over a four-year period, provided they make specific disclosures.

data.title: AI TRENDS | OpenAI Urges Investigation into Musk's Alleged Anti-Competitive Practices
data.description: OpenAI has called on authorities in California and Delaware to investigate alleged anti-competitive practices by Elon Musk. According to Jin10, the company is concerned about Musk's actions potentially stifling competition in the AI industry. OpenAI's request highlights ongoing tensions in the tech sector, where competition and innovation are closely monitored by regulatory bodies. The outcome of this investigation could have significant implications for the industry, particularly in how tech giants operate and collaborate.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Anthropic Projects Annual Revenue to Surpass $30 Billion in 2026
data.description: Anthropic, a leading AI company, has projected its annual revenue to exceed $30 billion by 2026. According to Jin10, this forecast marks a significant increase from the estimated $9 billion in revenue by the end of 2025. The company continues to expand its influence in the AI sector, reflecting its growth trajectory and market potential.

data.title: Goldman Sachs Revises 2026 Copper Price and Supply Surplus Forecasts
data.description: Goldman Sachs has adjusted its forecast for the average copper price in 2026 to $12,650 per ton, down from a previous estimate of $12,850. According to Jin10, the investment bank also anticipates a global copper market surplus of 490,000 tons in 2026, an increase from its earlier projection of 380,000 tons. These revisions reflect changes in market dynamics and supply expectations.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Broadcom Expands Collaboration with Google and Anthropic
data.description: Broadcom has announced an expansion of its collaboration with Google and Anthropic. According to Jin10, Anthropic is set to access 3.5 gigawatts of TPU artificial intelligence computing power starting in 2027. This development marks a significant step in enhancing AI capabilities and infrastructure for the involved companies.

data.title: Fed Officials Highlight Inflation Concerns Amid Energy Price Surge
data.description: On April 7, Jin10 reported that the ongoing conflict in Iran has led to a rise in energy prices, while the job market remains sluggish. Federal Reserve officials Hamack and Goolsbee have expressed that inflation is a more pressing issue than employment, suggesting their support for tightening rather than loosening monetary policy. In an interview, they were asked to assess the economic situation using colors, ranging from 'red' for urgent to 'green' for stable. Goolsbee described the inflation outlook as 'at least orange,' indicating a less optimistic scenario. He remarked, 'I was initially hopeful that we would return to the 2% inflation target, but recent developments have been disappointing.' Hamack also voiced concerns about inflation, noting that it has exceeded target levels for five consecutive years and has been 'essentially flat' over the past two years, describing the situation as a 'brighter, more vivid orange.' Regarding employment, Hamack expressed optimism, suggesting a yellow-green outlook, while Goolsbee rated the labor market as 'yellow.'

data.title: Bitcoin's Bear Flag Persists Amid Strategy's Aggressive Accumulation
data.description: Strategy has significantly increased its Bitcoin holdings, acquiring 46,233 BTC since March 2. According to NS3.AI, this accumulation contrasts with the production of approximately 16,200 BTC by miners during the same period, keeping Bitcoin within a bear flag pattern rather than confirming a breakdown. The company raised $102.6 million through STRC sales last week, contributing to a Bitcoin purchase exceeding $330 million. Analysts suggest that a breakout above the upper trendline, near the mid-$70,000 range, could invalidate the bearish setup and shift attention to a potential target between $108,000 and $110,000.

data.title: Nasdaq Leads Gains as Major U.S. Indices Rise
data.description: The Nasdaq Composite Index led the gains among major U.S. stock indices, rising by 0.54%. According to NS3.AI, the S&P 500 also experienced an increase, gaining 0.44%, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average added 0.36%. These movements reflect positive sentiment in the market, contributing to an overall upward trend in stock prices.

data.title: North Korean IT Workers Allegedly Involved in DeFi Development
data.description: Taylor Monahan has claimed that North Korean IT workers have contributed to the development of SushiSwap and other decentralized finance (DeFi) projects. According to NS3.AI, Monahan mentioned that these workers have been involved in the sector for several years. The projects cited include THORChain, Yearn, Harmony, Anchor, and Shiba Inu.

data.title: USDC Treasury Mints 250 Million USDC
data.description: According to NS3.AI, Whale Alert has reported the minting of 250 million USDC at the USDC Treasury. This development highlights ongoing activities within the stablecoin market, reflecting the demand and strategic movements in digital currency reserves.

data.title: Bitcoin Core Developers to Demonstrate 'Attack Blocks' on Signet Test Network
data.description: Bitcoin Core developers are set to showcase 'attack blocks' on Bitcoin's Signet test network at 10 AM EST on April 7. According to NS3.AI, these blocks are engineered to require significantly more time to verify compared to standard blocks. This demonstration aims to highlight a critical consensus vulnerability that the Great Consensus Cleanup seeks to resolve through BIP 54.

data.title: IMF Warns of Risks from Global Imbalances and Tariffs
data.description: IMF researchers have highlighted that tariffs rarely improve current accounts and are contributing to widening global imbalances, which pose risks to growth and financial stability. According to NS3.AI, the study indicates that permanent tariffs or retaliatory measures typically do not alter saving behaviors, leaving current account positions mostly unchanged. The IMF cautions that these growing imbalances have historically preceded financial crises or sudden reversals in capital flows.

data.title: The Iranian parliament's National Security Committee has begun deliberating on a plan to control the Strait of Hormuz.
data.description: Iranian Parliament's National Security Committee spokesman Ibrahim Rezaei said on the 6th that the committee has begun reviewing a proposal aimed at exercising Iranian sovereignty and establishing new arrangements and a legal framework for the Strait of Hormuz. The spokesman stated that a strategic action plan to ensure the security of the Strait of Hormuz and the Persian Gulf was on the agenda at this meeting, and some parts have already been reviewed and approved. After the National Security Committee completes its review, the proposal will be submitted to the plenary session of the Iranian Parliament for consideration. (CCTV)

data.title: Trump claims he destroyed Iranian bridge and power plant in 4 hours
data.description: On the afternoon of April 6 local time (early morning of April 7 Beijing time), US President Trump stated at a White House press conference that the US military "has actually developed a plan that, once activated, within four hours from 8 p.m. to midnight on April 7 Eastern Time, every bridge in Iran will be completely destroyed, every power plant in Iran will be completely paralyzed, engulfed in flames, exploded, and rendered unusable forever. Everything will be reduced to rubble, and the entire process will only take four hours." (CCTV International News)

data.title: Iran Reviews Plan for Legal Framework in Strait of Hormuz
data.description: Iran's Parliament National Security Committee has initiated the review of a proposal aimed at establishing sovereignty and a legal framework for the Strait of Hormuz. According to NS3.AI, spokesperson Ibrahim Rezaei stated that the plan encompasses strategic measures to ensure the security of both the Strait of Hormuz and the Persian Gulf. Upon completion of the review, the committee intends to forward the plan to the full parliament for further consideration.

data.title: BitMine Immersion Technologies Increases Ethereum Holdings with Significant Purchase
data.description: BitMine Immersion Technologies has acquired 71,252 Ethereum, valued at approximately $152 million, in the week ending April 5. According to NS3.AI, this acquisition has increased BitMine's total Ethereum holdings to 4.803 million, which is valued at around $10.3 billion and represents 3.98% of the circulating supply. The company has expressed its intention to accumulate 5% of the circulating supply. Following this purchase, shares of BMNR rose by about 6% on Monday.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Announces Potential Actions Against Iran
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has announced that all bridges in Iran are set to be destroyed by midnight on Tuesday. According to Odaily, he also stated that all power plants in the country will cease operations before midnight. If an agreement with Iran is not reached, these power plants will be dismantled. Trump emphasized that he hopes to avoid such actions.

data.title: Trump Criticizes NATO and Allies Over Iran Conflict Assistance
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has expressed dissatisfaction with South Korea, Australia, Japan, and NATO regarding their lack of support in the ongoing conflict with Iran. According to NS3.AI, Trump labeled NATO as a "paper tiger," criticizing the alliance for its hesitance to provide assistance.

data.title: JPMorgan Maintains Sell Rating on Tesla with $145 Price Target
data.description: JPMorgan has reaffirmed its sell rating on Tesla, setting a price target of $145, which suggests a potential downside of approximately 60% from current levels. According to NS3.AI, Tesla's reported deliveries for the first quarter of 2026 amounted to 358,023 vehicles, falling short of Wall Street's expectations.

data.title: IMF Chief: War Impacts Economic Growth Projections
data.description: IMF Managing Director Kristalina Georgieva stated that without the ongoing war, the IMF had initially planned to slightly raise its 2026 economic growth forecast of 3.3%. According to Jin10, Georgieva emphasized that current circumstances indicate a trend towards higher prices and slower growth.
